warning.title: Unofficial translation
warning.body: This translation is community-provided and may be inaccurate. Spotted a mistake? Please report it or open a fix on {repoLink}.

@block.description: Generic fallback NOUN for a board item whose title is unknown (a service / module / task node). Not the verb 'to block'.

@close.description: Verb meaning dismiss / shut (a panel, drawer, dialog), NOT the adjective 'near'. Used as an aria-label on close affordances - translate as the imperative verb.

@sandbox.description: Named feature area (a screen for trying prompt versions / models against graded fixtures). Fine to localize descriptively per locale - unlike nav.kaizen, this one is NOT kept verbatim.

@kaizen.description: Proper-noun product feature name (grading history + verified prompt/agent/model combos). Do NOT translate - keep 'Kaizen' verbatim in every locale.

@decisionWord.description: Count-based plural noun rendered AFTER a number, e.g. '3 decisions' (pending human decisions in the run queue). Resolved via t(key, count); count is always 1 or more. Provide ALL plural forms your language needs (English has 2; Polish/Ukrainian need 3 - one/few/many - and rely on the custom pluralRules wired in i18n.config.ts).

canvas.emptyBody: Add a service to get started: bootstrap a fresh repo or pull in one you already have.
canvas.dropOntoTaskBody: Pipelines run against tasks, not services.

addTask.title: Add a task
addTask.recurringWithFrame: A recurring task runs a pipeline on a cadence. Continue to set the schedule + prompt.
addTask.recurringNoFrame: A recurring task must live on a service. Add it from a service frame (or a module inside one).
addTask.notesPlaceholder: Add anything else the agent should know — appended to the issue description above…
addTask.descriptionPlaceholder: Describe the work — context, acceptance criteria, anything the agent should know…
addTask.technicalHint: A refactor / non-functional / internal change. The implementer treats the task definition as primary and the spec as a regression reference; leave off to let the spec phase decide.
addTask.stepsToReproducePlaceholder: Observed vs expected, and how to reproduce…
addTask.outlineHintsPlaceholder: Sections or points the document should cover
addTask.noDocsHint: Attach a requirement, RFC or PRD so agents see it while implementing this task.
addTask.noIssuesHint: Attach a tracker issue so agents see its description and comments while implementing this task.
addTask.plannedHint: The task is added in a planned state. It won't run until you start a pipeline on it — you can keep editing it until then.
addTask.linkFailed: Task added, but {count} attachment could not be linked | Task added, but {count} attachments could not be linked

@linkFailed.description: Count-based: how many context attachments (docs/issues) failed to link after the task was created (count is always >= 1). Provide ALL plural forms your language needs (English has 2; Polish/Ukrainian need 3 - one/few/many - via the custom pluralRules in i18n.config.ts).

recurring.title: Add a recurring pipeline
recurring.name: Name
recurring.promptPlaceholder: What should each run do? Describe the work — the same prompt a normal task carries. Leave blank to use the pipeline's default.
recurring.issueTrackerHint: The tech-debt pipeline files a ticket from its analysis before implementing. Choose where (saved for the whole workspace).
recurring.footerHint: A single recurring task is added inside the service; each run replaces the last. Its run history is visible in the inspector.

stop.label: Stop
stop.stoppedDescription: The container was killed and the run was cancelled.

frame.sharedTitle: Shared across workspaces in this org

@taskCount.description: Count-based task tally rendered as e.g. '3 tasks' (count is always >= 0). Provide ALL plural forms your language needs (English has 2; Polish/Ukrainian need 3 - one/few/many - via the custom pluralRules in i18n.config.ts).

@moduleCount.description: Count-based module tally rendered as e.g. '2 modules' (count is always >= 1). Provide ALL plural forms your language needs (English has 2; Polish/Ukrainian need 3 - one/few/many - via the custom pluralRules in i18n.config.ts).

task.dragToConnect: Drag onto another task to make it depend on this one
task.noPipelineBody: Create one in the builder first.
task.clickToViewStep: Click to view step details & output

@tester_infra_unsupported.description: Conflict-toast title for the Tester step's start gate. UMBRELLA over two cases not visible from the string: (a) the service has no test infra configured, AND (b) this deployment's container runtime can't run local test infra (limited mode). The detailed description carries the specific cause; keep the title broad ('not configured / unavailable').

@run_not_retryable.description: 'Run' is a NOUN here = a pipeline execution (only a failed run can be retried). Translate it as the execution noun, not the verb 'to run'.

conflict.fallbackMessage: This action conflicts with the current state.

providersUnconfigured.title: No AI provider for this model
providersUnconfigured.body: No provider is configured for {models}. Add a provider key, connect a subscription, or enable Cloudflare AI to run it.

@body.description: Keep the named placeholder for the model list intact (a comma-separated list of model ids is injected at runtime).

personalSubscriptions.heading: Personal subscriptions (individual use only)
personalSubscriptions.intro: Claude (Pro/Max), GLM (Z.ai Coding Plan) and ChatGPT (Codex) are each licensed to you as an individual, so they are stored just for you and only your runs use them. Each token is encrypted under a personal password (never stored) that you enter when you start such a run; it is cached in this browser for a while so it stays out of your way. The password is a low-friction convenience and a transparency signal that the system will not silently share your credential, not a wall against a system-key holder. These models cannot be used on recurring schedules.

renewal.expired: Your {vendor} subscription has expired. Renew it and reconnect to keep running its models.
renewal.soon: Your {vendor} subscription renews in {count} day. Update it here once renewed. | Your {vendor} subscription renews in {count} days. Update it here once renewed.

@soon.description: Count-based renewal reminder; {count} is the number of days until renewal (always >= 1). Provide ALL plural forms your language needs (English has 2; Polish/Ukrainian need 3 - one/few/many - via the custom pluralRules in i18n.config.ts). {vendor} is the subscription's product name, kept verbatim.

claude.label: Claude (Pro/Max)
claude.step1: Install Claude Code and sign in with your Claude Pro/Max account: run `claude` once and complete the browser login.
claude.step2: Generate a long-lived token: run `claude setup-token` and copy it.
claude.step3: Paste it below and choose a personal password to protect it.

glm.label: GLM (Z.ai Coding Plan)
glm.tokenPlaceholder: your GLM Coding Plan API key
glm.step1: Open your Z.ai GLM Coding Plan dashboard and create an API key for the Anthropic-compatible endpoint.
glm.step2: The GLM Coding Plan is licensed to you as an individual, so it is stored per-user here (not pooled).
glm.step3: Paste the key below and choose a personal password to protect it.

codex.label: ChatGPT (Codex)
codex.step1: Install the Codex CLI and sign in with your ChatGPT account: run `codex login` and complete the browser flow.
codex.step2: Open the credentials file Codex wrote at ~/.codex/auth.json (on Windows %USERPROFILE%\.codex\auth.json).
codex.step3: Copy the entire contents of auth.json, paste it below, and choose a personal password to protect it.

container.noTasks: No tasks yet. Add one to start work.
container.servicesHint: Services are long-lived and don't complete. Work happens in their tasks and modules.

epicChildren.title: Epic tasks
epicChildren.empty: No tasks belong to this epic yet. Import an epic's children, or set a task's epic.

fragments.serviceEmpty: None. Code-aware agents on this service follow their default guidance.

releaseHealth.title: Post-release health
releaseHealth.connectPrompt: Connect an observability integration to watch this service after releases.
releaseHealth.hint: The monitors and SLOs the gate watches after this service ships. Comma-separate ids.

testConfig.title: Test infrastructure
testConfig.defaultEnvHint: The default tasks under this service are spawned with. Each task can override it in its agent settings.

env.ephemeralHint: tests run against a provisioned env
env.localHint: the Tester stands deps up locally

testConfig.browseRepo: Browse the repository for the compose file
testConfig.composeHint: Used by the Tester to stand up the service's dependencies locally.
testConfig.selectComposeHint: Pick the compose file in the repository. Its path is stored relative to the repo root.
testConfig.noInfra: No infra dependencies (the Tester spins nothing up)
testConfig.missingInfra: Set a docker-compose path or enable no infra dependencies, otherwise a pipeline with a Tester won't start.
testConfig.provisioningHint: A hint for provisioning this service's ephemeral test environment: which cloud provider to deploy to and how large an instance to request. Ignored for local (docker-compose) testing.

dependencies.title: Depends on
dependencies.empty: No dependencies. Can run any time.

execution.stopTooltip: Stop the run but keep it (readable and retryable)
execution.resetTooltip: Discard this run and reset the task to planned
execution.fragmentsTooltip: Best-practice fragments folded into this step: {fragments}

structure.modulePlaceholder: e.g. Sessions (created on implement if new)
structure.bestPracticesEmpty: None. Agents follow their default guidance.

runSettings.pipelineEmpty: No default. Pick a pipeline when you run this task.
runSettings.mergePresetDetail: {name}: auto-merge when complexity ≤ {complexity}, risk ≤ {risk}, impact ≤ {impact}; up to {attempts} CI-fix attempts.
runSettings.mergePresetEmpty: No preset configured. The merger raises a review notification for every PR.
runSettings.modelPresetEmpty: No preset configured. Agents run on the deployment's default routing.
runSettings.unavailableModels: Not available under the current configuration: {models}. This task would fail on those steps.
runSettings.modelPresetChangeHint: Changing this affects only steps that haven't started yet.

technicalHint.technical: Technical: the implementer treats the task definition as primary and the spec as a regression reference; the spec-writer may produce no business specs.
technicalHint.business: Business: the specification leads, as usual.
technicalHint.unset: Auto-detect: inferred from the spec phase. Set it explicitly to override.

runSettings.writebackHint: Writes back to this task's linked tracker issue. Overrides the workspace default.
runSettings.responsibleEmpty: Unassigned. Set a product owner to notify them when requirement review flags this task.
runSettings.autoStartHint: When this task merges, automatically start the tasks that depend on it (once their other dependencies are also done).

decision.title: Decision required
decision.visualizationHint: This is a visualization. Any choice simply resumes the pipeline.

structuredResult.noResultHint: The structured output appears once this agent finishes. While it runs, the step shows live progress on the board.

stepDetail.companionCapHeading: {agent} hit its {attempts}-attempt rework limit, still below the {threshold} bar.
stepDetail.companionCapDetail: Do one more automatic rework round, proceed to the next step accepting the current output, or stop and reset the task so you can edit the inputs and resubmit.
stepDetail.editConclusionsPlaceholder: Edit the agent's conclusions; your edits are saved when you approve…
stepDetail.noProseOutput: This agent produced no prose output.
stepDetail.editHint: Edit the conclusions on the left; your edits are saved when you approve.
stepDetail.reviewHint: Click any block in the output to comment on it, or leave overall feedback below.
stepDetail.editingNotice: You're editing the conclusions directly. Manual edits can't be combined with per-block comments. Approve to save them, or cancel to return to review.
stepDetail.commentPlaceholder: Leave a comment on this block…
stepDetail.overallFeedbackPlaceholder: Describe the changes the agent should make (optional if you left per-block comments)…
stepDetail.rejectConfirmPrompt: Reject this proposal and stop the run entirely?
stepDetail.requestChangesHint: Request changes re-runs this step with your feedback & comments. Reject stops the run entirely.

inspector.taskStartedLocked: This task has started. Its details are locked.
inspector.priorDocHint: From the previous review. Use it as a base: edit the description above and resubmit.

observability.exportHint: Download an LLM-friendly JSON export of this run
observability.noCalls: No model calls recorded for this run.
observability.noContext: No agent context stored for this run. It is captured per dispatch when the workspace has 'Store full agent context' enabled.

metricsBar.cachedTokensHint: Prompt tokens served from the provider's cache
metricsBar.truncatedCalls: {count} call truncated at the limit | {count} calls truncated at the limit

localMode.noPatBody: Set GITHUB_PAT or GITLAB_PAT in your .env to sign in with a personal access token, then restart the server.
localMode.failed: Sign-in failed. Check that the configured token is valid and try again.

signInRequired.personalSubscriptions: Personal subscriptions are saved to your account, but this deployment runs without sign-in, so they can't be stored here. Use the workspace pool or a provider API key instead.
signInRequired.userApiKeys: Your personal API keys are saved to your account, but this deployment runs without sign-in, so they can't be stored here. Switch the scope to "This workspace" to add a shared key.

@appTitle.description: The product/app title shown on the login screen. Keep verbatim across every locale - it is a proper-noun-ish product name, do NOT translate it.

login.subtitle: Sign in to continue.
login.forgotSent: If an account exists for that email, a password reset link is on its way. Check your inbox.
login.signInFailed: Sign-in failed. Check your details and try again.
login.genericError: Something went wrong. Please try again.

resetPassword.title: Reset password
resetPassword.subtitle: Choose a new password for your account.
resetPassword.doneBody: Your password has been reset. You can now sign in with your new password.
resetPassword.missingToken: This reset link is missing its token. Request a new link from the sign-in screen.
resetPassword.errorTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters.
resetPassword.errorInvalidLink: This password reset link is invalid or has expired.

accountDeployment.title: Deployment integrations
accountDeployment.intro: Credentials shared by every workspace in this account, sealed at rest. Values are never shown after saving; leave a field blank to keep the stored secret.

slack.title: Slack app (OAuth)
slack.description: Enables the "Add to Slack" OAuth flow. Without it, workspaces can still connect Slack by pasting a bot token.
slack.validation: Enter the client id, secret and redirect URL

linear.title: Linear app (OAuth)
linear.description: Enables the "Connect with Linear" OAuth flow. Without it, workspaces can still connect Linear by pasting a personal API key.
linear.validation: Enter the client id, secret and redirect URL

web.title: Container web search
web.description: The search upstream container agents reach through the backend proxy. Set a Brave key (recommended), or a self-hosted SearXNG URL (with an optional bearer key).
web.validation: Enter a Brave key or a SearXNG URL
web.saveFailed: Could not save web search keys
web.clearFailed: Could not clear web search keys

contentStorage.title: Content storage
contentStorage.description: Where UI-tester screenshots and reference design images for this account are stored. The metadata always lives in the database; only the file bytes go to the chosen backend.
contentStorage.keysHint: Keys are sealed at rest and never shown after saving; leave blank to keep the stored keys.
contentStorage.regionBucketValidation: Enter the S3 region and bucket
contentStorage.bothKeysValidation: Enter both the access key id and secret
contentStorage.keysValidation: Enter the S3 access key id and secret

accountFragments.intro: Best-practice guidelines shared by every board in this account. Each board inherits these and can override or add its own. Code-aware agents (coder, reviewer, architect, fixers) fold the relevant ones into their prompt.

org.ctaBody: Members, roles and invitations live on an organization. Create one to invite teammates and manage their roles; your personal boards stay as they are.

invite.title: Invite a teammate
invite.createdEmailed: An email was sent (or copy the link from the list below).
invite.createdShareLink: Share the accept link with your teammate.

email.title: Email sender
email.notEnabled: Email delivery is not enabled on this deployment. Invitations still produce a shareable accept link.

setup.title: No AI model configured
setup.body: Agents need a model to run. AI works out of the box only on a Cloudflare deployment with Workers AI enabled. Otherwise connect a provider key, subscription, proxy, or local runner to continue.

preset.title: Default model preset uses unavailable models
preset.body: Tasks using the default preset would fail. Edit the preset or configure the missing provider.

githubPatBanner.title: GitHub PAT not configured
githubPatBanner.body: Local mode reaches GitHub with a personal access token. Without one, agent steps that clone, push, open PRs, gate on CI or merge will fail.

providerConfigBanner.body: A provider is wired for this instance but is missing required settings, so it can't run yet. Add the mandatory fields to finish connecting it.

spendWarningBanner.title: Spend limit reached, agent execution paused
spendWarningBanner.body: This month's token spend has hit the configured budget, so running pipelines were paused to avoid further cost. Execution resumes automatically when the budget rolls over next month, or once the limit is raised.
spendWarningBanner.resumeHint: Resuming continues spending; it will pause again if still over budget.

personalSetup.title: My setup
personalSetup.intro: Your personal connections, used for runs you start and visible only to you.

githubToken.label: My GitHub token
githubToken.description: A personal access token used for runs you start (pushes, PRs, CI, merge).

localRunners.label: My local runners
localRunners.description: Your own-machine model runners (Ollama, LM Studio, vLLM).

subscriptions.label: My subscriptions
subscriptions.description: Individual-usage plans you unlock with a personal password (Claude, GLM, Codex).

keywords.fragments: prompt fragments best practice guidelines context
keywords.workspaceSettings: limit running tasks per service waiting escalation overdue notification timeout
keywords.modelConfiguration: model llm routing agent kind default preset configuration
keywords.serviceFragmentDefaults: fragment best practice guideline service default code-aware
keywords.accountSettings: account team members roles invitations email api keys fragment best practice library context organization personal
keywords.localModels: local model runner ollama lm studio llamacpp vllm endpoint
keywords.sandbox: sandbox prompt model test experiment judge fixture benchmark evaluate

integrationsHub.title: Integrations
integrationsHub.intro: Connect and manage the external systems this workspace can link in.

getStarted.title: Get started
getStarted.body: Connect a code source and a model provider to run your first pipeline.

openrouter.description: One gateway to 300+ models. Add your key and enable models in one place.

vendors.label: Vendors and keys
vendors.description: Workspace LLM subscriptions and provider API keys.

github.description: Connect the workspace's GitHub App, browse repos, PRs and issues.

slack.description: Route notifications to your team's Slack workspace.

documentSource.description: Link {source} as a document source for requirements context.

documentImport.label: Import and spawn
documentImport.description: Pull documents from a connected source and spawn structure.

taskSource.description: Link {source} to import and reference tracker issues.

taskImport.label: Import issues
taskImport.description: Pull issues from a connected tracker onto the board.

observability.label: Post-release health
observability.description: Watch monitors and SLOs after a release ships (Datadog).

githubPat.label: My GitHub token
githubPat.description: A personal access token used for runs you start (pushes, PRs, CI, merge).

localRunners.label: My local runners
localRunners.description: Your own-machine model runners (Ollama, LM Studio, vLLM…).

modelConfiguration.title: Model Configuration
modelConfiguration.subtitle: Presets that map a model to every agent. A task picks one; tasks default to the workspace default preset.

list.intro: Each preset sets a {baseModel} for every agent, with optional per-agent overrides. A model pinned on an individual task still overrides the preset.
list.deleteDisabledTitle: The default preset cannot be deleted
list.empty: No presets yet. Create one to map models to your agents.

toast.setDefaultFailed: Could not set the default preset

infrastructure.registerHint: Register a runner pool below to enable this.
infrastructure.updateFailed: Could not update the execution backend

executionBackend.label: Where agents run
executionBackend.local-dockerDesc: Run each agent in a container on this machine's Docker daemon.
executionBackend.cloudflare-containersDesc: Run agents on Cloudflare's built-in per-run container platform.
executionBackend.kubernetesDesc: Run each agent as a pod in a Kubernetes cluster you operate.
executionBackend.runner-poolDesc: Dispatch agents to your own scheduler behind an HTTP manifest API.
executionBackend.k3sDesc: Prefilled for a local k3s/k3d cluster on this machine — just paste a token.

testEnvBackend.label: Where test environments run
testEnvBackend.local-composeDesc: Stand the Tester's dependencies up with docker-compose inside the run's container.
testEnvBackend.kubernetesDesc: Provision a per-PR namespace in a Kubernetes cluster you operate.
testEnvBackend.environment-providerDesc: Provision ephemeral environments through your own HTTP management API.

providerConnection.noneAvailable: No infrastructure providers are enabled on this deployment.

environment.title: Ephemeral environment provider
environment.blurb: Where the Tester agent runs against a live preview environment. Configure the per-workspace settings and credentials your provider needs.

runner-pool.title: Agent runner backend
runner-pool.blurb: Where the coding agents run when not using Cloudflare Containers. Choose a self-hosted runner pool (your own scheduler) or a Kubernetes cluster, then configure its endpoint and credentials.

kubernetesEnv.label: Name
kubernetesEnv.apiServerUrlHelp: The kube-apiserver root, e.g. https://10.0.0.1:6443. The orchestrator applies each PR's manifests through the apiserver, so only this endpoint must be reachable.
kubernetesEnv.apiTokenHelp: A bearer token with RBAC to create namespaces and apply the operator's resources. Stored encrypted; never shown again.
kubernetesEnv.repoHelp: The owner/repo that holds the Kubernetes manifests, e.g. acme/preview-manifests.
kubernetesEnv.refHelp: Branch, tag or SHA to read the manifests at. Empty uses the repo's default branch.
kubernetesEnv.pathHelp: File or directory within the repo holding the resources to apply.
kubernetesEnv.hostTemplateHelp: Host template rendered with the provision vars such as branch and pullNumber; the rendered host becomes the environment URL.
kubernetesEnv.ingressNameHelp: Ingress to read the load-balancer host from. Empty uses the only Ingress applied.
kubernetesEnv.namespaceTemplateHelp: Per-PR namespace name rendered from the provision vars such as pullNumber. Empty derives one from the PR number.
kubernetesEnv.imageTemplateHelp: Image reference exposed to the manifests, rendered over the provision vars such as branch and sha.
kubernetesEnv.caCertPemHelp: Paste the cluster CA bundle so the apiserver's TLS certificate verifies. Omit only for a publicly-trusted CA.
kubernetesEnv.insecureSkipTlsVerifyHelp: Strongly discouraged. Disables apiserver TLS verification; use only for kind/dev clusters.

kubernetes.label: Name
kubernetes.apiServerUrlHelp: The kube-apiserver root, e.g. https://10.0.0.1:6443. The orchestrator reaches each run's pod through the apiserver, so only this endpoint must be reachable.
kubernetes.imageHelp: The executor-harness image each per-run pod runs.
kubernetes.apiTokenHelp: A bearer token with RBAC to create, get and delete pods and pods/proxy in the namespace. Stored encrypted; never shown again.
kubernetes.caCertPemHelp: Paste the cluster CA bundle so the apiserver's TLS certificate verifies. Omit only for a publicly-trusted CA.
kubernetes.insecureSkipTlsVerifyHelp: Strongly discouraged. Disables apiserver TLS verification; use only for local k3s/kind/dev clusters.

manifestEditor.title: Provider manifest
manifestEditor.jsonHelp: Describe your provider's API declaratively: the base URL, auth scheme, request templates, and the response mapping. Validated against the same contract the server enforces.
manifestEditor.invalidJson: The manifest is not valid JSON.
manifestEditor.invalidShape: The manifest does not match the expected shape.
manifestEditor.reenterSecrets: Re-enter every secret to save. Stored secrets are write-only and aren't shown.
manifestEditor.starterHint: This is a starter example. Edit it to match your provider's API.

form.reenterSecrets: Re-enter the secret field to save changes — stored secrets are write-only and aren't shown. | Re-enter the secret fields to save changes — stored secrets are write-only and aren't shown.

@reenterSecrets.description: Count-based: how many write-only secret fields the user must re-supply before re-saving (count is always >= 1). Provide ALL plural forms your language needs (English has 2; Polish/Ukrainian need 3 - one/few/many - via the custom pluralRules in i18n.config.ts).

serviceFragmentDefaults.intro: Pick the best-practice fragments every new service starts with. Their guidance is folded into the prompt of every code-aware agent (coder, reviewer, architect, fixers) on the service's tasks. You can refine the set per service in its inspector; changing this default does not affect services that already exist.
serviceFragmentDefaults.empty: None — new services start with no service-level fragments.

footer.question: Need to add or edit the fragments themselves (hand-authored, linked docs, repos)?

filing.heading: Where tickets are filed
filing.description: The tech-debt recurring pipeline raises an issue before implementation and files it in this tracker. Choose {none} to skip filing.
filing.githubHint: GitHub Issues rides your installed GitHub App, which isn't connected yet. Install it under {link} — filing stays off until then.
filing.jiraHint: Jira isn't connected yet. {link} to file and link issues.
filing.linearHint: Linear isn't connected yet. {link} to file and link issues.
filing.jiraProjectKeyHelp: New tickets are filed under this project.
filing.linearTeamIdHelp: New issues are created under this team (Linear requires a team to create an issue).

linking.heading: Link issues as context
linking.description: When a source is offered you can import its issues and attach them to a task, so agents see the issue description and comments while implementing. This is independent of the filing tracker above.

github.available: Rides your installed GitHub App — no credentials needed.
github.unavailable: Install the GitHub App (Integrations → GitHub) to offer this source.

jira.connectHint: Connect with a Jira account and API token.

linear.connectHint: Connect with a Linear personal API key.

writeback.heading: Writeback
writeback.description: Write back to a task's linked issue(s) as its pull request progresses. Each toggle is the workspace default and can be overridden per task in the inspector. GitHub issues close natively; Jira issues transition to the first status in their {done} category.

commentOnOpen.label: Comment when a PR opens
commentOnOpen.help: Post a comment on the linked issue with the new pull request's link.

resolveOnMerge.label: Close as resolved when a PR merges
resolveOnMerge.help: Comment that the PR merged, then close / resolve the linked issue.

probeFailure.title: Issue tracking isn't available
probeFailure.disabled: The task-source integration is turned off on this deployment (its encryption key is not configured). Set ENCRYPTION_KEY on the backend to enable issue tracking.
probeFailure.serverError: The issue-tracker service returned an error (HTTP {status}): {message}. This usually means the backend isn't fully migrated/configured.

userSecrets.title: My GitHub token
userSecrets.intro: Store a personal access token used for the runs {you} start — pushes, pull requests, the CI gate and merges are attributed to your GitHub access. Stored {scope}; the token is write-only and never shown again.

mergeThresholds.intro: Named auto-merge policies a task can choose. After CI passes, the {merger} agent scores the PR on complexity, risk and impact (0-100%); the PR auto-merges only when every score is at or below the preset's ceilings, otherwise a review notification is raised. The default preset governs any task that picks none.
mergeThresholds.deleteDefaultBlocked: The default preset cannot be deleted

observabilityConnection.title: Post-release health
observabilityConnection.intro: After a release ships, the {gate} gate watches the configured observability monitors/SLOs. On a regression it spawns an on-call agent to investigate (a human decides whether to revert). Map which monitors/SLOs a service watches from that service's inspector.

incident.heading: Incident enrichment
incident.description: Optional. On a regression, the on-call investigation is posted onto an incident these systems ALREADY opened from the same signals (annotate, never re-alert). Secrets are write-only: blank leaves a stored value unchanged.

localMode.title: Local mode
localMode.intro: Tuning for the local container runner, stored on this machine's deployment (it replaced the {poolVars} / {harnessVars} env vars). Saving resizes the warm pool live, no restart needed; in-flight runs keep the container they already hold.

pool.heading: Warm container pool
pool.description: Keep idle harness containers ready and re-lease one (preferring a container that already holds the run's repo) instead of cold-starting per run. Pool size 0 disables it. Requires a Docker-family runtime (Docker/Podman/OrbStack/Colima); ignored on Apple {appleContainer}.

size.label: Pool size
size.help: Max idle warm containers. 0 = pooling off.

minWarm.label: Pre-warm at boot
minWarm.help: Containers started when the service boots.

max.label: Max containers
max.help: Hard cap (leased + idle). Blank = pool size.

idleTimeout.label: Idle timeout (minutes)
idleTimeout.help: Evict an idle pooled container after.

checkout.heading: Checkout reuse
checkout.description: When a warm container already holds the run's repo, the harness reuses its per-repo checkout (clean sweep + fetch + switch branch) instead of cloning fresh.

workspaceRoot.label: Workspace root
workspaceRoot.help: Absolute in-container directory the reused checkout lives under.

cleanKeep.label: Keep on clean (comma-separated)
cleanKeep.help: Dependency-cache directories the per-run clean sweep preserves.

openRouterCatalog.intro: Reach {models} through one gateway. Add your OpenRouter key below, then enable the models you want. They appear in the model picker with their context window and price, and meter against your spend budget.
openRouterCatalog.step1: Open {link} and create an API key.
openRouterCatalog.step2: Copy the key (starts with {prefix}) and paste it below.
openRouterCatalog.empty: No models yet. Hit {action} to load OpenRouter's live list.

waiting.heading: Waiting for a human
waiting.body: A run parked on a human decision (a review, an approval, a merge) waits as long as it needs and is never cancelled. After this many minutes its notification turns red and is flagged {overdue} in the inbox.

taskLimit.heading: Running tasks per service
taskLimit.body: Cap how many tasks may run at once under one service. Starting a task over the limit is refused with a clear message until a running task finishes.

observability.heading: Agent observability
observability.body: Store the complete context provided to each agent: the composed prompts, the best-practice fragments folded in, and the full content of the files injected into its container, so it can be inspected later in the observability view. The bodies are kept for the same window as the per-call LLM telemetry. Turn off to stop storing it (existing snapshots are pruned by retention).

retention.heading: Screenshot retention
retention.body: How long to keep the UI tester's captured screenshots and the reference design images they're reviewed against (the visual-confirmation gate). A daily cleanup job deletes both the image bytes and their metadata once they age past this window.

kaizen.heading: Kaizen agent
kaizen.body: After each run completes, the Kaizen agent grades how every agent step went, from smooth and efficient to confused and chaotic, and recommends prompt/model improvements. A prompt + agent + model combination that grades highly with no recommendations five times in a row is marked verified and is no longer graded. Grading runs in the background and is shown inside run details and the Kaizen screen. Set the grader's model in Model Configuration (the "Kaizen" agent).

@body.description: "Kaizen" is a product feature name; keep it verbatim in every locale.

budget.heading: Monthly spend budget
budget.body: Token usage is metered per LLM call, priced, and gated by this budget. When reached, runs in this workspace pause and the board shows a warning. Leave blank to inherit the built-in default (about 100 EUR/month).

localModelEndpoints.title: My local runners
localModelEndpoints.intro: Point agents at an LLM running on {ownMachine} (Ollama, LM Studio, llama.cpp, vLLM, or any OpenAI-compatible server). A runner is stored {justForYou} (a runner lives on your box), and the models you enable appear automatically in the model picker. The API key (most runners ignore it) is write-only and never shown again.
localModelEndpoints.apiKeyKeepPlaceholder: leave blank to keep stored key
localModelEndpoints.reachable: Reachable · no models | Reachable · {count} model | Reachable · {count} models

presetMismatch.title: Preset uses unavailable models
presetMismatch.intro: The workspace default model preset "{name}" assigns models that aren't available under the current configuration. Tasks that use this preset would fail when they reach those steps.
presetMismatch.advice: Either repoint the preset at models you have configured, or add the missing provider.

onboarding.title: Set up an AI model provider
onboarding.noModelTitle: No AI model is available on this workspace yet.
onboarding.noModelBody: Agents need a model to run. AI works out of the box only on a Cloudflare deployment with Workers AI enabled; otherwise connect at least one source below.
onboarding.operatorNote: AWS Bedrock and Cloudflare Workers AI are enabled by the deployment operator via environment configuration, not from this screen.

keys.title: Provider keys & subscriptions
keys.body: Add a direct provider API key (OpenAI, Anthropic, Qwen, …) or connect a commercial coding-plan subscription (Kimi, DeepSeek) or a personal one (Claude, GLM, Codex).

openrouter.title: OpenRouter gateway
openrouter.body: Enable models through the OpenRouter gateway with a single key. Browse and turn on the models you want.

local.title: My local runners
local.body: Point cat-factory at a model you run yourself (Ollama, LM Studio, llama.cpp, vLLM, …). No API key, no spend.

personalCredential.connectBody: This task uses a {vendor} model, which runs on your own {vendor} subscription. Connect (or renew) it first, then start the run again.
personalCredential.passwordBody: Enter the personal password that protects your {vendor} subscription. It unlocks your credential for this run only and is cached in this browser so you won't be asked again for a while.

apiKeys.directAccountIntro: Connect a vendor API key shared by every workspace in this account. Keys are stored encrypted, pooled, and rotated by usage. Account keys are admin-managed.
apiKeys.directIntro: Connect a vendor API key so models run directly on that provider. Keys are stored encrypted, pooled, and rotated by usage. Scope a key to this workspace (shared with the team) or to you (your own pool, usable anywhere).
apiKeys.proxyAccountIntro: Connect a proxy key (OpenRouter, LiteLLM) shared by every workspace in this account. A proxy is an intermediary that fronts many vendors behind a single key. Keys are stored encrypted, pooled, and rotated by usage.
apiKeys.proxyIntro: Connect a proxy key (OpenRouter, LiteLLM). A proxy is an intermediary that reaches many vendors' models through a single gateway, rather than one vendor directly. Keys are stored encrypted, pooled, and rotated by usage. Scope a key to this workspace (shared with the team) or to you (your own pool, usable anywhere).
apiKeys.cachingNote: Enables prompt caching for {provider} models, so a long multi-turn run reuses its cached prompt prefix instead of re-sending it every turn.
apiKeys.usage: {tokens} tok this window · {count} call | {tokens} tok this window · {count} calls

@usage.description: Usage readout for a connected API key. {tokens} is a preformatted token count; {count} is the number of API calls in the window and drives the plural. Provide ALL plural forms your language needs (English has 2; Polish/Ukrainian need 3 - one/few/many - via the custom pluralRules in i18n.config.ts).

openai.label: OpenAI
openai.step1: Open platform.openai.com → API keys and create a new secret key.
openai.step2: Copy the key (starts with sk-…); it is shown only once.

anthropic.label: Anthropic (Claude API)
anthropic.step1: Open console.anthropic.com → Settings → API Keys and create a key.

qwen.label: Qwen (Alibaba DashScope)
qwen.step1: Open the DashScope console (international) → API-KEY and create a key.
qwen.step2: Copy the key; it authenticates the OpenAI-compatible Qwen endpoint.

deepseek.label: DeepSeek
deepseek.step1: Open platform.deepseek.com → API keys and create a key.

moonshot.label: Moonshot (Kimi API)
moonshot.step1: Open platform.moonshot.ai → API Keys and create a key.
moonshot.step2: Copy the key; it authenticates the OpenAI-compatible Moonshot endpoint.

openrouter.label: OpenRouter
openrouter.step1: Open openrouter.ai → Keys and create an API key.
openrouter.step2: Copy the key (starts with sk-or-…); it reaches 300+ models through one gateway.

litellm.label: LiteLLM (self-hosted gateway)
litellm.step1: Generate a virtual key on your LiteLLM gateway (or use its master key).
litellm.step2: The gateway's base URL is set by your deployment operator (LITELLM_BASE_URL), not here.

vendorCredentials.title: LLM Vendors
vendorCredentials.poolIntro: Connect a commercial coding-plan subscription (Kimi, DeepSeek) that permits team/organization use to run agent steps on the Claude Code harness instead of an API key. Tokens are stored encrypted, pooled, and rotated by usage. Subscription models are flat-rate quota, so they don't draw on your spend budget. Individual-use subscriptions (Claude, GLM, ChatGPT/Codex) are connected per-user in the Personal subscriptions tab.
vendorCredentials.usage: {tokens} tok this window · {count} run | {tokens} tok this window · {count} runs

@usage.description: Usage readout for a connected subscription token. {tokens} is a preformatted token count; {count} is the number of runs in the window and drives the plural. Provide ALL plural forms your language needs (English has 2; Polish/Ukrainian need 3 - one/few/many - via the custom pluralRules in i18n.config.ts).

kimi.label: Kimi (Moonshot coding plan)
kimi.step1: Open your Moonshot (Kimi) coding-plan console and create an API key for the Anthropic-compatible endpoint.
kimi.step2: Copy the API key. Agent steps will run via Claude Code against Moonshot's Anthropic endpoint with full context.

deepseek.label: DeepSeek (coding plan)
deepseek.step1: Open your DeepSeek coding-plan console and create an API key for the Anthropic-compatible endpoint.
deepseek.step2: Copy the API key. Agent steps will run via Claude Code against DeepSeek's Anthropic endpoint with full context.

onboarding.title: Connect cat-factory to GitHub
onboarding.intro: cat-factory works by opening pull requests on your repositories. Install the GitHub App on your account or organization to continue, and you can grant it every repository or pick a subset.

connect.noInstallations: No installations found for the App yet. Install it below, or connect by ID.

errors.startInstall: Could not start the GitHub App install

panel.connectIntro: Connect a GitHub App installation to back board blocks with repositories, browse pull requests and issues, and let agents push branches and open PRs.
panel.manageHint: Pick the repositories this board should track. The GitHub connection is shared across the account, and each board links its own repos.
panel.noAvailableRepos: The installation can't access any repositories yet.
panel.noLinkedRepos: No repositories linked yet. Use Manage repos to pick which repositories this board tracks.

addService.title: Add a service from a repository
addService.intro: Pick an existing GitHub repository to add as a board service. No bootstrapping: the repo is linked to a new service frame as-is, and tasks you run on it target that repo.
addService.connectFirst: Connect this workspace to GitHub first. Link an installation the App is already on, or install it.
addService.repositoryHint: Repositories the GitHub App can access. Don't see yours? Grant the App access below, then refresh.
addService.noReposAvailable: No repositories available yet. Grant the App access to one below, then refresh.
addService.searchPlaceholder: Search repositories by owner or name…
addService.searchMinChars: Type at least {min} character to search. | Type at least {min} characters to search.

@searchMinChars.description: Shown when a large repo list needs a typed query before searching. Resolved via t(key, { min }, min) so {min} also drives the plural choice; min is always 2 or more. Provide ALL plural forms your language needs (English has 2; Polish/Ukrainian need 3 - one/few/many - and rely on the custom pluralRules wired in i18n.config.ts).

addService.monorepoLabel: This is a monorepo (hosts more than one service)
addService.monorepoDescription: Add several services from one repo, each pinned to a subdirectory.
addService.monorepoBrowseHint: Browse the repository and pick the directory of the service you want to add. Agents working on this service will run within that subdirectory.
addService.grantAccess: Grant the App access to a repo
addService.grantAccessTitle: Open the App's installation settings to grant it access to a repository

toast.addedDescription: {title} is on the board, configure it below.

panel.title: Slack notifications
panel.intro: Post board notifications (merge reviews, pipeline completions, CI failures) to Slack. The connection is shared across the account; routing is per board.

members.heading: Member map (user id to Slack member id)
members.hint: {product} people are mentioned on requirement-review findings; everyone else only when they created the task.

picker.searchPlaceholder: Search pages or paste a URL/ID…
picker.refPlaceholder: Paste a page URL or ID…
picker.emptySearchable: Search by title, or pick an imported document.
picker.emptyRefOnly: Paste a page URL or ID to attach it.

connect.title: Connect source
connect.intro: Connect {source} to import requirements, RFCs and PRDs, then spawn board structure or attach them to tasks as agent context.

taskDocs.heading: Context documents
taskDocs.empty: Attach a requirement, RFC or PRD so agents see it while implementing this task.

picker.searchPlaceholder: Search issues or paste a URL/key…
picker.refPlaceholder: Paste an issue URL or key…
picker.emptySearchable: Search by title, or pick an imported issue.
picker.emptyRefOnly: Paste an issue URL or key to attach it.

contextIssues.title: Context issues
contextIssues.emptyHint: Attach a Jira issue so agents see its description and comments while implementing this task.

import.connectFirst: Connect or enable a task source first.
import.asEpicTitleReady: Spawn this epic + its children as a linked task group
import.asEpicTitleNeedsContainer: Pick a container for the child tasks first
import.searchPlaceholder: Search by title, paste an issue URL, or type an issue number…
import.needFrameFirst: Add a service frame to the board first to create tasks from issues.
import.createFromIssue: Create a task from this issue
import.emptyHint: Search above, or paste an issue URL/key to create a task from it.
import.epicChildren: {count} child task created | {count} child tasks created

@epicChildren.description: Count-based: how many child tasks were created when spawning an epic (count is always >= 1). Provide ALL plural forms your language needs (English has 2; Polish/Ukrainian need 3 - one/few/many - via the custom pluralRules in i18n.config.ts).

connect.title: Task source
connect.intro: {label} lets you import issues and attach them to tasks as agent context.
connect.credentialless: This source uses the GitHub App already installed on your workspace, so there are no credentials to enter.
connect.installAppHint: Install the workspace's GitHub App (connect GitHub repos) to offer {label}.
connect.offerHint: When off, {label} is hidden from import and linking.
connect.oauthOr: or connect with an API key

builder.title: Pipeline builder
builder.configureModelsTooltip: Manage model presets (which model each agent runs on)
builder.gatingNeedsEstimator: A gated step needs a Task Estimator before it. Add one or the pipeline won't save.
builder.emptyDraft: Click agents on the left to assemble a linear pipeline.
builder.companionAdd: Add the {companion} (reviews this step, loops it back below threshold)
builder.disableTooltip: Disable this step (kept in the pipeline but skipped at run)
builder.enableTooltip: Step disabled (skipped at run). Click to enable.
builder.approvalAddTooltip: Require human approval after this step
builder.approvalRemoveTooltip: Approval required after this step. Click to remove the gate.
builder.consensusEnableTooltip: Enable consensus (multi-model panel/debate/voting) for this step
builder.consensusRevertTooltip: Consensus enabled. Click to revert to a single agent.
builder.followUpEnableTooltip: Follow-up companion disabled. Click to enable (Coder surfaces loose ends / questions).
builder.followUpDisableTooltip: Follow-up companion enabled. Coder surfaces loose ends / side-tasks / questions; click to disable.
builder.removeStep: Remove this step from the pipeline
builder.companionGateTooltip: Only run this companion when the task estimate clears a threshold (needs a Task Estimator earlier)
builder.consensusGateTooltip: Only run consensus when the task estimate clears a threshold (else the standard agent runs)
builder.cloneDefault: Clone this default into an editable copy

toast.addOneFirst: Add at least one agent first

progress.clickToRead: Click to read this agent's output

health.title: Pipeline health
health.allValid: All pipelines are valid and up to date.
health.invalidDescription: These reference a missing agent or are misconfigured, so they would fail (or misrun) at start. Delete a custom pipeline, or reseed a built-in to restore its catalog definition.
health.updatesDescription: A newer version of these built-in pipelines has shipped. Reseed to adopt it (your labels and archive state are kept).

palette.hint: Click an agent to append it to the pipeline.

subtitle.humanReview: Waits for a human code review on the PR, looping the fixer on comments
subtitle.ci: Gates the PR on green CI, looping the CI fixer on failure
subtitle.conflicts: Gates the PR on a clean merge, looping the resolver on conflicts

gates.noActivityHint: The precheck runs once the PR is open. While it polls, the step shows live state on the board.
gates.passedConflicts: The PR merges cleanly with its base.

humanReview.requestFixDescription: Describe a change for the fixer to make on the PR branch now (in addition to any review comments, which it addresses automatically).
humanReview.requestFixPlaceholder: e.g. rename the helper and add a unit test for the empty-input case

ci.failureFallback: CI has not reported a failure on this commit.

sidebar.footer: A gate runs a programmatic precheck and only spins up the {helper} when it fails; a green check advances with nothing spun up.

requirements.title: Requirements review
requirements.intro: An AI reviewer inspected this {level}'s collected requirements — its description plus any linked PRDs and tracker issues — and raised the findings below. {answer} the relevant ones and {dismiss} the irrelevant, then incorporate them; the reviewer re-reviews until the requirements are clear.
requirements.empty: No review yet. The reviewer runs automatically as the first step when this task's pipeline starts.
requirements.settled: The requirements are settled. The document below is what every downstream agent step uses.

exceeded.heading: Reached the {max}-iteration limit with findings still open.
exceeded.detail: Do one more review round, proceed to the next phase with the last incorporated requirements anyway, or stop and reset the task so you can rework the requirements and resubmit.

working.incorporating: Incorporating your answers into a requirements document… You can close this — we'll notify you only if more input is needed.
working.reReviewing: Re-reviewing the updated requirements… You can close this — we'll notify you only if more input is needed.

requirements.recommendationRequested: Recommendation requested — review the suggestion below.

actions.requestRecommendations: Request {count} recommendation | Request {count} recommendation | Request {count} recommendations

help.canProceed: Every finding is dismissed — proceed to the next phase without reworking.
help.canIncorporate: Folds your answers into one standard-format document, then re-reviews it automatically.
help.answerAll: Answer or dismiss every finding to continue.
help.merged: Re-review runs the reviewer against this document. If you're unhappy with how it was merged, redo it with a comment instead.

requirements.redoPlaceholder: What should the merge do differently?
requirements.settledFooter: Requirements settled — the pipeline is continuing with the document on the left.

toast.preparingRecommendations: Preparing {count} recommendation in the background | Preparing {count} recommendation in the background | Preparing {count} recommendations in the background
toast.preparingRecommendationsDescription: Your answers are saved — close this if you like; we'll notify you when they're ready.
toast.recommendationsReady: {count} recommendation ready | {count} recommendation ready | {count} recommendations ready
toast.incorporating: Incorporating your answers in the background
toast.incorporatingDescription: You're back on the board — we'll notify you only if more input is needed.
toast.reviewerSatisfied: Reviewer is satisfied — continuing the pipeline
toast.iterationLimitReached: Iteration limit reached — choose how to proceed
toast.newFindings: no new findings to react to | {count} new finding to react to | {count} new findings to react to
toast.taskReset: Task reset — edit the requirements and resubmit

clarity.title: Bug-report triage
clarity.intro: An AI reviewer triaged this {level}'s bug report for fixability, its description plus any linked context, and raised the questions below. {answer} the relevant ones and {dismiss} the irrelevant, then incorporate them; the reviewer re-reviews until the bug report is clear enough to fix.

@intro.description: Sentence with named slots: {level} is the block type (frame/module/task or 'item'), {answer} and {dismiss} are styled verb spans rendered via i18n-t. Keep {answer}/{dismiss} as the imperative verbs.

@introAnswer.description: Imperative verb 'answer' (respond to a question), used as an inline emphasized span in the intro sentence.

@introDismiss.description: Imperative verb 'dismiss' (wave off as irrelevant), used as an inline emphasized span in the intro sentence.

clarity.empty: No review yet. The reviewer runs automatically as the first step when this task's pipeline starts.
clarity.converged: The bug report is clarified. The report below is what every downstream agent step uses.
clarity.capHeading: Reached the {max}-iteration limit with findings still open.
clarity.capDetail: Do one more review round, proceed to the next phase with the last clarified bug report anyway, or stop and reset the task so you can rework the bug report and resubmit.
clarity.incorporatingStage: Incorporating your answers into a clarified bug report… You can close this, we'll notify you only if more input is needed.
clarity.reReviewingStage: Re-reviewing the updated bug report… You can close this, we'll notify you only if more input is needed.

@answerLabel.description: Noun label heading the recorded reply to a finding (the answer that was given), not the verb.

hint.proceed: Every finding is dismissed, proceed to the next phase without reworking.
hint.incorporate: Folds your answers into one clarified bug report, then re-reviews it automatically.
hint.answerAll: Answer or dismiss every finding to continue.

clarity.redoPlaceholder: What should the merge do differently?
clarity.redoHint: Re-review runs the reviewer against this report. If you're unhappy with how it was merged, redo it with a comment instead.
clarity.incorporatedFooter: Bug report clarified, the pipeline is continuing with the report on the left.

toast.clarifyingTitle: Clarifying the bug report in the background
toast.clarifyingDescription: You're back on the board, we'll notify you only if more input is needed.
toast.reReviewSatisfied: Reviewer is satisfied — continuing the pipeline
toast.reReviewExceeded: Iteration limit reached — choose how to proceed
toast.reReviewNewFindings: no new findings to react to | one new finding to react to | {count} new findings to react to

@reReviewNewFindings.description: Plural by {count} of newly raised findings after a re-review. Polish/Ukrainian need three forms (one/few/many).

toast.taskReset: Task reset — edit the bug report and resubmit

error.reReview: Could not re-review the bug report

consensus.empty: No consensus session has run for this step yet.

brainstorm.intro: An AI partner proposed the {subject} options below, each with its trade-offs. {choose} the ones you want (and steer them) and {dismiss} the rest, then incorporate; it re-runs until you converge on a {doc}.
brainstorm.empty: No brainstorm yet. It runs automatically when this task's pipeline reaches the brainstorm step.
brainstorm.settledBanner: The {doc} is settled. The document below is what the next stage uses.

exceeded.heading: Reached the {max}-iteration limit with options still open.
exceeded.detail: Do one more brainstorm round, proceed to the next phase with the last direction anyway, or stop and reset the task so you can edit it and resubmit.

working.incorporating: Folding your choices into a {doc}… You can close this — we'll notify you only if more input is needed.
working.reReviewing: Re-running the brainstorm on the updated direction… You can close this — we'll notify you only if more input is needed.

hint.allDismissed: Every option is dismissed — proceed to the next phase without a direction.
hint.incorporate: Folds your choices into one {doc}, then re-runs the brainstorm automatically.
hint.chooseAll: Choose or dismiss every option to continue.

brainstorm.redoPlaceholder: What should the direction do differently?
brainstorm.mergedHint: Re-run runs the agent against this direction. If you're unhappy with how it was merged, redo it with a comment instead.
brainstorm.incorporatedFooter: Direction settled — the pipeline is continuing with the document on the left.

toast.draftingDescription: You're back on the board — we'll notify you only if more input is needed.
toast.reReviewExceeded: Iteration limit reached — choose how to proceed
toast.reReviewNewOptions: no new options to react to | one new option to react to | {count} new options to react to

@reReviewNewOptions.description: Count of newly raised brainstorm options the user must react to; uses plural forms.

toast.taskReset: Task reset — edit it and resubmit

spec.title: Requirements
spec.error: Could not load the specification. Try reopening this window.
spec.noGherkinForGroup: No Gherkin scenarios for this group.
spec.moduleHint: no modules. Select a feature group on the left to view its requirements. | 1 module. Select a feature group on the left to view its requirements. | {count} modules. Select a feature group on the left to view its requirements.
spec.moduleHintGherkin: no modules. Select a feature group on the left to view its requirements or switch to the Gherkin scenarios. | 1 module. Select a feature group on the left to view its requirements or switch to the Gherkin scenarios. | {count} modules. Select a feature group on the left to view its requirements or switch to the Gherkin scenarios.

empty.title: No specification yet
empty.description: This service has no spec on its default branch. A specification is generated as tasks run their pipelines; once it lands on main it will appear here, with its Gherkin scenarios.

@given.description: Gherkin keyword that opens a precondition clause. Keep verbatim — it is literal Gherkin syntax, not prose.

@when.description: Gherkin keyword that opens an action clause. Keep verbatim — it is literal Gherkin syntax, not prose.

@then.description: Gherkin keyword that opens an expected-outcome clause. Keep verbatim — it is literal Gherkin syntax, not prose.

followUp.title: Follow-ups
followUp.subtitle: Forward-looking follow-ups and questions the Coder surfaced. The pipeline continues once every item is decided.

badge.toDecide: {count} to decide | {count} to decide | {count} to decide

empty.title: No follow-ups yet.
empty.hint: As the Coder works it streams loose ends, side-tasks and questions here. They appear live, so you can act on them before the Coder even finishes.

followUp.answerPlaceholder: Answer this question — it's folded into the Coder's next pass…

@footer.summary.description: Item count drives the plural form; {undecided} is a separate interpolated count of still-undecided items, not the plural driver.

humanTest.title: Human testing
humanTest.subtitle: Validate the change in a live environment

fix.heading: Found a problem?
fix.placeholder: Describe what went wrong — the Fixer agent gets this as context, then the environment is rebuilt for re-testing.

history.heading: History (no rounds) | History (one round) | History ({count} rounds)

testing.title: Test report
testing.subtitle: Exploratory + regression testing of the change

empty.title: No test report yet.
empty.hint: The report appears once the Tester finishes a pass. While it runs, the step shows live progress on the board.

testing.noDiscreteCheck: No discrete check recorded for this scenario.
testing.footer: Scenarios are the areas the Tester chose to exercise (its spec acceptance scenarios). Outcomes and concerns are grouped under them by name.

@screenshotAlt.description: Alt text for a captured screenshot thumbnail; {view} is the screen/view name. The literal word 'screenshot' should be localized.

visualConfirm.title: Visual confirmation
visualConfirm.subtitle: Review the UI against the reference designs
visualConfirm.noScreenshots: No screenshots were captured. Review the change manually, or upload a reference below.

upload.heading: Upload a reference design
upload.tipReady: Tip: drop an image straight onto a pair above to set its reference.
upload.tipNeedView: Enter a view name first, then choose a file. Or drop an image straight onto a pair above.

requestFix.heading: Request a fix
requestFix.placeholder: Anything else the Fixer should know (in addition to any per-view notes above).
requestFix.foldedHint: Per-view notes are folded in automatically.

history.heading: History (no rounds) | History ({count} round) | History ({count} rounds)

focus.emptyPipelineHint: Run a pipeline to visualize the agents working on this block.
